title: MemGPT: Towards LLMs as Operating Systems
snippet: Packer et al., 2023. OS-inspired virtual memory management for LLMs. Two-tier architecture: main context (RAM) and external context (disk). Self-editing memory through function calls. Core memory (always accessible), recall memory (searchable database), archival memory (long-term). Foundation for Letta.

metadata.llm_reason: Foundational memory architecture paper, OS-inspired virtual context management
metadata.author: Charles Packer et al.

title: Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning
snippet: Shinn et al., 2023. Agents reflect on task feedback via verbal self-evaluation, storing reflections in episodic memory for subsequent trials. NeurIPS 2023. Key innovation: linguistic reinforcement and episodic memory buffer.

metadata.llm_reason: Pioneered episodic memory buffer pattern for agent self-improvement
metadata.author: Noah Shinn et al.

title: Voyager: An Open-Ended Embodied Agent with Large Language Models
snippet: Wang et al., NVIDIA, 2023. Skill library pattern: agent stores verified code as reusable skills in long-term memory. Automatic curriculum generation. First open-ended learning agent with procedural memory.

metadata.llm_reason: Landmark agent with procedural memory via skill library
metadata.author: Guanzhi Wang, Jim Fan et al.

title: DSPy: Programming—not prompting—Language Models
snippet: Khattab et al., Stanford. Declarative LM programs compiled into optimized prompt pipelines. Memory of optimized prompts persists across runs. Self-improving prompt optimization via teleprompters.

metadata.llm_reason: Framework with persistent prompt memory and self-optimization
metadata.author: Omar Khattab et al.

title: ReAct: Synergizing Reasoning and Acting in Language Models
snippet: Yao et al., 2022. Interleaved reasoning traces and actions. Working memory pattern: the reasoning trace serves as in-context working memory for action selection. ICLR 2023.

metadata.llm_reason: Foundational pattern using reasoning traces as working memory
metadata.author: Shunyu Yao et al.

title: Tree of Thoughts: Deliberate Problem Solving with Large Language Models
snippet: Yao et al., 2023. Multiple reasoning paths with self-evaluation and backtracking. Search tree serves as external memory of explored solutions. NeurIPS 2023.

metadata.llm_reason: Explicit memory of explored reasoning paths via tree structure
metadata.author: Shunyu Yao et al.

title: Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback
snippet: Bai et al., Anthropic. Self-critique against constitutional principles stored as persistent rules. Memory of principles guides iterative revision. Foundation for RLAIF.

metadata.llm_reason: Persistent principle memory driving self-revision loop
metadata.author: Yuntao Bai et al., Anthropic

title: LangChain: Build context-aware reasoning applications
snippet: Framework with ConversationBufferMemory, ConversationSummaryMemory, ConversationKnowledgeGraphMemory. Multiple memory backend patterns. Ecosystem includes LangGraph for stateful agent workflows.

metadata.llm_reason: Major framework implementing multiple memory architecture patterns
metadata.author: LangChain AI

title: AutoGen: Multi-Agent Conversation Framework
snippet: Microsoft Research. Multi-agent framework with shared conversation memory. Agents share context through conversation history. GroupChat pattern for collective memory.

metadata.llm_reason: Multi-agent shared memory via conversation history
metadata.author: Microsoft Research

title: Self-Taught Optimizer (STOP): Recursively Self-Improving Code Generation
snippet: Zelikman et al., 2023. Recursive self-improvement through code rewriting. Memory of previous program versions drives optimization. LLMs as mutation operators on their own code.

metadata.llm_reason: Recursive self-improvement with version memory
metadata.author: Eric Zelikman et al.

title: Language Agent Tree Search (LATS)
snippet: Zhou et al., 2023. MCTS with LLM-based value functions and self-reflection. Experience memory from failed attempts guides future search. Combines reasoning, acting, and planning.

metadata.llm_reason: Experience memory from failed attempts improves future planning
metadata.author: Andy Zhou et al.

title: Self-Instruct: Aligning Language Models with Self-Generated Instructions
snippet: Wang et al., 2022. LLMs bootstrap instruction-following by self-generating instruction-output pairs. Training data as memory: model learns from its own generated examples.

metadata.llm_reason: Self-generated training data as form of accumulated memory
metadata.author: Yizhong Wang et al.

title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP Tasks
snippet: Lewis et al., Meta AI, 2020. RAG combines parametric memory (LLM weights) with non-parametric memory (retrieved documents). Foundational architecture for external memory in LLMs. NeurIPS 2020.

metadata.llm_reason: Foundational dual-memory architecture: parametric + non-parametric
metadata.author: Patrick Lewis et al., Meta AI

title: Generative Agents: Interactive Simulacra of Human Behavior
snippet: Park et al., Stanford, 2023. Generative agents with memory stream architecture: observation, reflection, planning. Memory retrieval by recency, importance, relevance. Foundational work on structured agent memory.

metadata.llm_reason: Pioneered memory stream with recency-importance-relevance retrieval
metadata.author: Joon Sung Park et al., Stanford

title: Generative Agents: Interactive Simulacra of Human Behavior (UIST 2023)
snippet: Full version. Memory stream stores observations, reflections generated through self-prompting summarize patterns, and plans are hierarchical action schedules. Three retrieval factors: recency (exponential decay), importance (LLM-scored), relevance (embedding similarity).

metadata.llm_reason: Detailed memory stream architecture with 3-factor retrieval
metadata.author: Joon Sung Park et al.

title: An Empirical Study of Catastrophic Forgetting in Large Language Models During Continual Fine-tuning
snippet: Luo et al., 2023. Empirical study showing LLMs suffer catastrophic forgetting during sequential fine-tuning. Forgetting worsens as model size grows (1B-7B). Key risk for memory architectures relying on weight updates.

metadata.llm_reason: Documents catastrophic forgetting risk in LLM memory via weight updates
metadata.author: Yun Luo et al.

title: Letta (formerly MemGPT) - Agent Framework with Persistent Memory
snippet: UC Berkeley spin-off, $10M seed from Felicis. Production framework for agents with hierarchical memory (core/recall/archival). Open-source. Commercial platform for enterprise agent memory.

metadata.llm_reason: Commercial framework directly implementing MemGPT memory architecture
metadata.author: Letta Inc

title: Mem0: Memory Layer for AI Applications
snippet: YC-backed startup. $24M raised. Processes 186M+ API calls per quarter. Stores, retrieves, and evolves user memory across models and platforms. Compatible with OpenAI, Anthropic, open-source LLMs.

metadata.llm_reason: Leading commercial memory layer with massive production adoption
metadata.author: Mem0 Inc

title: Zep: Context Engineering and Agent Memory Platform
snippet: Temporal knowledge graph architecture for agent memory. Graphiti engine captures evolving interactions with bi-temporal tracking (event time + ingestion time). Open-source core. 94.8% accuracy on DMR benchmark vs 93.4% for MemGPT. 90% latency reduction.
query: Zep context engineering temporal knowledge graph memory agent 2025

metadata.llm_reason: Temporal knowledge graph approach to agent memory, outperforms MemGPT
metadata.author: Zep Inc

title: Zep: A Temporal Knowledge Graph Architecture for Agent Memory
snippet: Rasmussen et al., 2025. Temporal knowledge graph with Graphiti engine. Bi-temporal tracking: event time T and ingestion time T'. Dynamic fact invalidation and supersession. 18.5% accuracy improvement, 90% latency reduction vs baselines.
query: Zep context engineering temporal knowledge graph memory agent 2025

metadata.llm_reason: Novel temporal knowledge graph architecture for agent memory
metadata.author: Preston Rasmussen et al.

title: MAGMA: A Multi-Graph based Agentic Memory Architecture for AI Agents
snippet: UT Dallas + University of Florida, 2026. Four orthogonal graphs: semantic, temporal, causal, entity. Policy-guided traversal for retrieval. 45.5% higher reasoning accuracy on long-context benchmarks, 95% token reduction, 40% faster latency.

metadata.llm_reason: State-of-the-art multi-graph memory architecture with dramatic improvements
metadata.author: Fred Jiang et al.

title: A-MEM: Agentic Memory for LLM Agents
snippet: Xu et al., 2025. Zettelkasten-inspired agentic memory. Dynamic indexing and linking. Each memory item gets contextual descriptions, keywords, tags. Historical memory connections enable memory evolution. NeurIPS 2025.

metadata.llm_reason: NeurIPS 2025 paper on Zettelkasten-inspired self-organizing memory
metadata.author: Wujiang Xu et al.

title: Agent Memory Paper List: Memory in the Age of AI Agents Survey
snippet: Curated paper list for 'Memory in the Age of AI Agents' survey. Comprehensive collection of memory architecture papers organized by type: working memory, episodic, semantic, procedural.
query: episodic semantic procedural memory AI agent architecture 2025 2026

metadata.llm_reason: Comprehensive curated survey of agent memory architectures
metadata.author: Shichun Liu et al.

title: A Survey on the Memory Mechanism of Large Language Model-based Agents
snippet: ACM TOIS 2025. First comprehensive survey on memory mechanisms in LLM agents. Covers working memory (in-context), short-term memory (conversation buffer), long-term memory (retrieval-augmented). Taxonomy of memory operations: read, write, reflect.

metadata.llm_reason: First comprehensive ACM survey on LLM agent memory mechanisms
metadata.author: ACM TOIS authors

title: From Storage to Experience: A Survey on the Evolution of LLM Agent Memory Mechanisms
snippet: 2026 survey. Three evolutionary stages: Storage (trajectory preservation), Reflection (trajectory refinement), Experience (trajectory abstraction). Framework for understanding memory system maturity.

metadata.llm_reason: Latest 2026 survey with evolutionary framework for memory mechanisms
metadata.author: Preprints.org authors

title: Continuum Memory Architectures for Long-Horizon LLM Agents
snippet: 2026. Addresses memory for long-horizon agent tasks. Continuum approach rather than discrete memory tiers. Novel architecture for sustained agent operation over extended timelines.

metadata.llm_reason: Novel continuum memory approach for long-horizon agents
metadata.author: arXiv authors

title: LoCoMo: Evaluating Very Long-Term Conversational Memory of LLM Agents
snippet: Snap Research benchmark. Multi-session conversations with 25 events over 6-12 months, 32 sessions, ~600 turns. Tests factual recall, multi-hop reasoning, temporal reasoning. Standard benchmark for memory evaluation.
query: LLM memory benchmark evaluation LOCOMO MemBench 2025

metadata.llm_reason: Standard benchmark for evaluating long-term conversational memory
metadata.author: Snap Research

title: MemoryBench: A Benchmark for Memory and Continual Learning in LLM Systems
snippet: 2025. Benchmark for memory systems focusing on information extraction, multi-hop reasoning, knowledge updating, preference following, temporal reasoning. Evaluates both storage and retrieval quality.

metadata.author: arXiv authors

title: LifeBench: A Benchmark for Long-Horizon Multi-Source Memory
snippet: 2026. Benchmark for long-horizon memory from multiple sources. Tests agent ability to integrate and reason over memories accumulated across diverse interactions over time.

metadata.llm_reason: Latest 2026 benchmark for multi-source long-horizon memory
metadata.author: arXiv authors

snippet: 2026. Survey on memory for autonomous agents. Covers memory types, storage mechanisms, retrieval strategies, and evaluation frameworks for production agent systems.

snippet: ICLR 2026 workshop proposal. Dedicated workshop on memory for LLM agents. Signals field maturity — memory architectures have become a distinct research area with its own community.

title: Mechanistic Analysis of Catastrophic Forgetting in LLMs During Continual Fine-tuning
snippet: 2026. Identifies three mechanisms: gradient interference in attention weights, representational drift in intermediate layers, loss landscape flattening. Attention heads 15-23% in lower layers show severe disruption.
